Examples

C API

Typedefs

typedef double (*form_factor_f)(double x, void *ctx)

Functions

int hankel_transform(int nu, form_factor_f f, double *x, size_t len_x, void *f_ctx, double *output, const char *strategy_name, strategy_params strategy_params)

Computes Hankel transform using the method specified by the user.

Note

The parameter strategy_name can be any of “QWE_Chave”, “QWE_Key”, “DHT_6”, “DHT_7”, “DHT_8”, “DHT_9”, “DHT_10”, “DHT_11”.

Parameters:

  • nu – order of bessel function - must be 0 or 1

  • f – pointer to function to transform

  • x – pointer to array of x at which to compute the transform

  • f_ctx – pointer to struct containing inputs for f

  • output – pointer to array containing output from transform

  • strategy_name – str corresponding to the strategy name

  • strategy_params – struct containing params for the specific strategy (please use the docs to find params required by each strategy)

double hankel_transform_DHT(int nu, form_factor_f f, const double x, void *f_ctx, double *output, int n_strategy)

Computes Hankel transform, using digital filters.

Note

Corresponds to strategies 6-11 in SASfit.

Note

Does not allow any error control.

Note

Perform wells for simple form factors, but it does struggle with oscillatory ones. For oscillatory form factors, it is advisable to start with this method for a rough Hankel transform computation, and then refine it with “hankel_transform_QWE_Chave” or “hankel_transform_QWE_Key”.

Parameters:

  • nu – order of bessel function - must be 0 or 1

  • f – pointer to function to transform

  • x – value at which to compute the transform

  • f_ctx – pointer to struct containing inputs for f

  • output – pointer to var containing output from transform

  • n_strategy – an integer number between 6 and 11 (determines which weightings to use for the transform and corresponds to SASfit strategies 6-11)

double compute_hankel_FBT(int nu, form_factor_f intern_fct, void *ctx, double x, int n_method, int n_eval, double f_max)
double hankel_transform_FBT(int nu, double (*f)(double, double (*)[50]), double x, double (*f_params)[50], double *output, int n_method, int n_eval, double f_max)
double hankel_transform_DE_Ooura(int nu, form_factor_f f, const double x, void *f_ctx, double *output, int n_eval, double eps_rel)

Computes Hankel transform, using de-quadrature.

Note

Corresponds to strategy 0 in SASfit, or HANKEL_OOURA_DEO.

Parameters:

  • nu – order of bessel function - must be 0 or 1

  • f – pointer to function to transform

  • x – value at which to compute the transform

  • f_ctx – pointer to struct containing inputs for f

  • output – pointer to var containing output from transform

  • n_eval – integer indicating number of function evaluations (N_ogata in SASfit)

  • eps_rel – relative error allowed e.g. 1e-9 (eps_nriq in SASfit)

double hankel_transform_DE_Ogata(int nu, form_factor_f f, const double x, void *f_ctx, double *output, int n_eval, double f_max)

Computes Hankel transform, using de-quadrature.

Note

Corresponds to strategy 1 in SASfit or HANKEL_OGATA_2005.

Parameters:

  • nu – order of bessel function - must be 0 or 1

  • f – pointer to function to transform

  • x – value at which to compute the transform

  • f_ctx – pointer to struct containing inputs for f

  • output – pointer to var containing output from transform

  • n_eval – integer indicating number of function evaluations (N_ogata in SASfit)

  • f_max – float indicating starting guess for max in form factor (h_ogata in SASfit)

double hankel_transform_QWE_Key(int nu, form_factor_f f, const double x, void *f_ctx, double *output, int n_eval, double eps_rel)

Computes Hankel transform using the Quadrature With Extrapolation method by Key.

Note

Corresponds to strategy 12 in SASfit.

Parameters:

  • nu – order of bessel function - must be 0 or 1

  • f – pointer to function to transform

  • x – value at which to compute the transform

  • f_ctx – pointer to struct containing inputs for f

  • output – pointer to var containing output from transform

  • n_eval – integer indicating number of function evaluations (N_ogata in SASfit)

  • eps_rel – relative error allowed e.g. 1e-9 (eps_nriq in SASfit)

double hankel_transform_QWE_Chave(int nu, form_factor_f f, const double x, void *f_ctx, double *output, int n_eval, double eps_rel)

Computes Hankel transform using the Quadrature With Extrapolation method by Chave.

Note

Corresponds to strategy 13 in SASfit.

Parameters:

  • nu – order of bessel function - must be 0 or 1

  • f – pointer to function to transform

  • x – value at which to compute the transform

  • f_ctx – pointer to struct containing inputs for f

  • output – pointer to var containing output from transform

  • n_eval – integer indicating number of function evaluations (N_ogata in SASfit)

  • eps_rel – relative error allowed e.g. 1e-9 (eps_nriq in SASfit)

struct strategy_params
#include <libhankel.h>

double eps_rel

struct form_factor_ctx
